mission.title: kubevela: [Feature] ApplicationDeployment CRD to support application level rollout, traffic, and multi-cluster strategies
mission.description: [Feature] ApplicationDeployment CRD to support application level rollout, traffic, and multi-cluster strategies. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kubevela deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kubevela version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
helm list -n kubevela 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working kubevela installation.

steps.title: Review kubevela configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubevela configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get configmap -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubevela
```
Currently Vela supports Application CRD which templates the low level resources and exposes high level parameters to users. But that's not enough. It requires a couple of standard techniques to deploy an application in production:

1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Feature] ApplicationDeployment CRD to support application…
steps.description:
ref: https://github.com/oam-dev/kubevela/issues/1144
```yaml
kind: ApplicationDeployment
name: example-app-deploy
spec:
  trafficRules:
    hosts:
      - example-app.example.com
    http:
      - match:
          - uri:
              prefix: "/web"
        weightedTargets:
          - revisionName: example-app-v1
            weight: 50
          - revisionName: example-app-v2
            weight: 50

  appRevisions:
    - # Name of the AppRevision.
      # Each modification to Application would generate a new AppRevision.
      revisionName: example-app-v1

      # Cluster specific workload placement config
      placement:
        - clusterSelector:
  
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubevela -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubevela
kubectl get events -n kubevela --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[Feature] ApplicationDeployment CRD to support application…" is functioning correctly.

codeSnippets:
kind: ApplicationDeployment
name: example-app-deploy
spec:
  trafficRules:
    hosts:
      - example-app.example.com
    http:
      - match:
          - uri:
              prefix: "/web"
        weightedTargets:
          - revisionName: example-app-v1
            weight: 50
          - revisionName: example-app-v2
            weight: 50

  appRevisions:
    - # Name of the AppRevision.
      # Each modification to Application would generate a new AppRevision.
      revisionName: example-app-v1

      # Cluster specific workload placement config
      placement:
        - clusterSelector:
            # You can select Clusters by name or labels.
            # If multiple clusters is selected, one will be picked via a unique hashing algorithm.
            labels:
              usage: produ

kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: prod-cluster-1
  labels:
    usage: production
spec:
  kubeconfig: ...

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
  kind: VirtualService
  spec:
    hosts:
      - example-app.example.com
    http:
      - match:
          - uri:
              prefix: "/web"

        route:
          - destination:
              host: example-app-v1
            weight: 50
          - destination:
              host: example-app-v2
            weight: 50

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubevela installed or the issue environment reproducible.
